title: Transaction Commitment Record v0.2
$comment: TCR extends the W3C PROV Activity model with a commitment lifecycle. W3C PROV provides vocabulary for activity/agent/entity provenance; it does not define commitment deadlines, lifecycle states, breach detection, or dispute windows. XACML 3.0 Obligations cover synchronous commitment semantics only. TCR's temporal lifecycle + portable dispute-ready evidence container are novel. See docs/standards-alignment.md.

commitment_id.description: Unique identifier for this commitment record.

transaction_id.description: Links this record to the transaction. Maps to W3C PROV Activity identifier.

issuer.description: The agent responsible for the commitments. Maps to W3C PROV Agent (wasAssociatedWith).

counterparty.description: The principal on whose behalf the agent acted. Maps to W3C PROV Agent (actedOnBehalfOf).

commitments.description: The obligations this transaction carries. Conceptually analogous to XACML 3.0 Obligations but persistent, portable, and time-aware. Each commitment has a deadline and a fulfillment state that is tracked over time.

type.description: Commitment type. Examples: approval, delivery, redaction, notification, audit_export.

due_by.description: Deadline for fulfillment. AGF extension beyond XACML Obligations: if this timestamp passes with fulfilled=false and required=true, the record MUST be treated as breached.

fulfilled.description: Whether this commitment has been fulfilled.

required.description: Whether this commitment is required for the transaction to be considered fulfilled. If required=true and fulfilled=false after due_by, status MUST be breached.

evidence_refs.description: References to artifacts that support this record. Maps to W3C PROV Entity references (used, wasGeneratedBy). Must be preserved through dispute_window_end.

kind.description: Artifact type. Examples: trace, log, receipt, attestation, snapshot.

ref.description: Reference to the artifact (URI, hash, or identifier).

status.description: Lifecycle state. AGF-specific lifecycle: pending → fulfilled/breached/revoked. A breached or revoked status blocks finalize/close actions. No equivalent lifecycle exists in W3C PROV or XACML.

breach_reason.description: Machine-readable breach cause. Required when status='breached'.

dispute_window_end.description: AGF-specific: evidence_refs MUST be preserved through this timestamp for dispute adjudication. No equivalent concept in W3C PROV or XACML.

audit_required.description: If true, the enforcement point MUST emit audit evidence for this record evaluation.
